description: CSRs do not have write access in user mode, except for user floating-point CSRs.
example_target: mstatus (example shown); applies to all 67 CSRs except user FP CSRs
notes: Covers the 67 CSRs of NS31A. See Table 1 for full CSR list.

description: CSRs can only be written by instructions with matching CSR address.
CSR can only be read by instructions with matching CSR address.
CSR read access is done only for CSR instructions with corresponding register and immediate operand as shown in Table 2.
notes: Refer to Table 2 for full operand behaviour. DESIGN BUG FOUND: see Counterexample 1.

description: CSR write access is done only for CSR instructions with corresponding register and immediate operand as shown in Table 2.
notes: Refer to Table 2 for full operand behaviour. DESIGN BUG FOUND: see Counterexample 2.

description: Read-only CSR's value is constant as it should not be modified in any case.
notes: Covers read-only CSRs: mvendorid, marchid, mimpid, mhartid, cycle, time, instret, cycleh, timeh, instreth.

description: CSR should be in a valid format. Some read/write CSR fields are only defined for a subset of bit encodings, but allow any value to be written while guaranteeing to return a legal value whenever read. Reserved bits in mie should be 0 (example).
example_target: mie (example shown); applies to all CSRs with reserved/restricted fields
notes: One property per CSR with format constraints.

description: Exception flag is set for write to a read-only CSR register.
Exception flag is set for attempts to access a non-existent CSR.
Exception flag is set for attempts to access a CSR without appropriate privilege level.
